question: Do office and remote employees have the same core collaboration window?
expected_answer: Core collaboration hours are 10:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m. local time, Monday through Thursday. Remote employees must be reachable during the core hours of 10:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m. in their local time zone, Monday through Thursday.
source_passage: Core collaboration hours are 10:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m. local time, Monday through Thursday. Weekly time records must be submitted by 6:00 p.m. local time each Friday.

Remote workers must use a private workspace or a headset that prevents confidential conversations from being overheard. Remote employees must be reachable during the core hours of 10:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m. in their local time zone, Monday through Thursday.

question: Compare the initial timing requirements for reporting a suspected security incident and acknowledging a Severity 1 engineering page.
expected_answer: A suspected information-security incident must be reported to the Security Operations Center within 30 minutes of detection. The primary on-call engineer must acknowledge a Severity 1 page within 5 minutes.
source_passage: A suspected information-security incident must be reported to the Security Operations Center within 30 minutes of detection. A suspected phishing message must be forwarded as an attachment to security@example.test without clicking links or replying.
The primary on-call engineer must acknowledge a Severity 1 page within 5 minutes. The primary on-call engineer must acknowledge a Severity 2 page within 15 minutes.

question: Which procurement and personnel records have a seven-year retention requirement, and when does each clock start?
expected_answer: Procurement records must be retained for 7 years after contract expiration or termination. A former employee's personnel file is retained for 7 years after termination of employment.
source_passage: Only an officer listed in the corporate authority matrix may sign a supplier contract; a project role alone does not grant signing authority. Procurement records must be retained for 7 years after contract expiration or termination.
A former employee's personnel file is retained for 7 years after termination of employment. Records for an unsuccessful job applicant are retained for 2 years after the hiring decision.

question: A planned trip will cost USD 6,000. What travel preapproval and procurement quote rules are stated in the documents?
expected_answer: Travel expected to exceed USD 5,000 requires written preapproval from the employee's director and Finance Operations. A purchase from USD 5,000 through USD 25,000 requires at least three competitive quotes.
source_passage: An expense report must be submitted within 10 business days after the traveler returns. Travel expected to exceed USD 5,000 requires written preapproval from the employee's director and Finance Operations.
A purchase below USD 5,000 requires one documented supplier quote. A purchase from USD 5,000 through USD 25,000 requires at least three competitive quotes.

question: Compare the review cadence for project risks with the cadence for business continuity tabletop exercises.
expected_answer: The project manager and core team must review the active risk register at least every two weeks. Each business unit must complete a tabletop continuity exercise every quarter.
source_passage: The project manager and core team must review the active risk register at least every two weeks. A change that increases approved cost or schedule by more than 10% requires steering committee approval before implementation.
Each business unit must complete a tabletop continuity exercise every quarter. Every Tier 1 service must complete a production failover test at least once each calendar year.

question: Compare the initial response or acknowledgement target for a Priority 1 support case and a Severity 1 engineering page.
expected_answer: A Priority 1 customer case has a 15-minute initial response target, available 24 hours a day and 7 days a week. The primary on-call engineer must acknowledge a Severity 1 page within 5 minutes.
source_passage: A Priority 1 customer case has a 15-minute initial response target, available 24 hours a day and 7 days a week. A Priority 2 customer case has an initial response target of 2 business hours.
The primary on-call engineer must acknowledge a Severity 1 page within 5 minutes. The primary on-call engineer must acknowledge a Severity 2 page within 15 minutes.

question: What waiting periods affect a new hire's regular remote-work eligibility and benefits enrollment?
expected_answer: A new employee becomes eligible for a regular remote schedule after 90 calendar days of employment and a satisfactory performance standing. A new employee has 30 calendar days from the start date to complete benefits enrollment.
source_passage: A new employee becomes eligible for a regular remote schedule after 90 calendar days of employment and a satisfactory performance standing. An approved hybrid employee may normally work remotely up to 3 days per workweek.
Health coverage begins on the first day of the month after the employee's start date. A new employee has 30 calendar days from the start date to complete benefits enrollment.

question: Compare the notification deadline after continuity-plan activation with the reporting deadline for a lost office badge.
expected_answer: Mass notification must begin within 15 minutes after the business continuity plan is activated. A lost access badge must be reported to Workplace Security within 15 minutes of discovery.
source_passage: The designated Incident Commander has authority to activate the business continuity plan. Mass notification must begin within 15 minutes after the business continuity plan is activated.
A lost access badge must be reported to Workplace Security within 15 minutes of discovery. A visitor in a secured office area must display a temporary badge and remain escorted by an employee host.

question: What governance applies to an initiative above USD 25,000 under the project and procurement documents?
expected_answer: A formal project charter is required when estimated effort exceeds 100 person-hours or total spend exceeds USD 25,000. A purchase above USD 25,000 requires a formal request for proposals managed by Strategic Sourcing.
source_passage: A formal project charter is required when estimated effort exceeds 100 person-hours or total spend exceeds USD 25,000. The accountable executive sponsor must approve the project charter before detailed planning begins.

A purchase above USD 25,000 requires a formal request for proposals managed by Strategic Sourcing. A purchase above USD 100,000 requires approval from both the Chief Financial Officer and the Procurement Director.

question: How soon must a support interaction be documented, and how long is the closed ticket retained?
expected_answer: A substantive customer interaction must be documented in the case record by the end of the same business day. A customer support ticket is retained for 3 years after the ticket is closed.
source_passage: A support agent may approve a goodwill credit up to USD 100; a support manager may approve up to USD 500. A substantive customer interaction must be documented in the case record by the end of the same business day.
A customer support ticket is retained for 3 years after the ticket is closed. A recorded customer support call is retained for 18 months after the recording date.

question: State the annual wellness allowance and the annual vendor-gift limit.
expected_answer: Eligible employees may receive up to USD 600 per calendar year for approved wellness expenses. Gifts from a single vendor may not exceed an aggregate value of USD 75 in a calendar year.
source_passage: Eligible employees may receive up to USD 600 per calendar year for approved wellness expenses. After 6 months of service, an employee may receive up to USD 5,000 per calendar year in approved tuition assistance.
A potential conflict of interest must be disclosed to Ethics within 5 business days of discovery. Gifts from a single vendor may not exceed an aggregate value of USD 75 in a calendar year.

question: Compare the update cadence for a Severity 1 continuity incident, engineering incident, and Priority 1 support case.
expected_answer: For a Severity 1 continuity incident, internal status updates must be issued at least every 60 minutes. Customer-facing status updates must be posted at least every 30 minutes during an active Severity 1 incident. The assigned case owner must update the customer at least every 60 minutes while a Priority 1 case remains active.
source_passage: Only the Crisis Communications Lead or a formally delegated spokesperson may make external statements about a continuity incident. For a Severity 1 continuity incident, internal status updates must be issued at least every 60 minutes.

Customer-facing status updates must be posted at least every 30 minutes during an active Severity 1 incident. A Severity 1 postmortem must be completed within 5 business days after service restoration.

The assigned case owner must update the customer at least every 60 minutes while a Priority 1 case remains active. A written resolution summary must be sent to the customer within 2 business days after case resolution.
